title: JUnit 5 and Mockito
learningObjectives: Apply **JUnit 5 and Mockito** with production-grade tests, tools, and measurable outcomes
Explain trade-offs between test types: unit, slice, integration, contract, e2e
Use profiling and benchmarking vocabulary correctly in senior interviews
Deliver behavioral answers with STAR structure tied to real architecture decisions
Run mock interviews with follow-up depth on testing and performance
Connect reliability practices from earlier phases to capstone scenarios

sections.title: Why JUnit 5 and Mockito matters
sections.content: **JUnit** **5** + **Mockito** are the **default** **Java** **unit** **testing** **stack**. Interviews expect **clean** **test** **doubles** and **verification** **discipline** without **over-mocking** **domain** **logic**.

sections.title: Theory and Internals - JUnit 5 and Mockito
sections.content:
### Day 85 — **JUnit** **5** & **Mockito**

### 1. Test pyramid
**Fast** **unit** **base**; **fewer** **heavy** **integration**.

### 2. Mockito basics
**Stub** **behavior**, **verify** **calls**, **avoid** **static** **pitfalls**.

### 3. JUnit 5
**Lifecycle**, **assertions**, **extensions**.

### 4. Design for test
**Ports** **enable** **mock** **repositories**.

### 5. Anti-patterns
**Testing** **implementation** **details** **only**.

### 6. Story
**Flaky** **test** fixed by **removing** **Thread.sleep** and **using** **Awaitility** or **deterministic** **fakes**.

sections.title: Basic — JUnit 5 smoke
Intermediate — Mockito vocabulary
Advanced — Verify interactions
Unit under test with mocks
Exercise — JUnit 5 and Mockito (Day 85 assignment)
sections.problem:
Print **exactly** two lines:
1. Implemented: JUnit 5 & Mockito
2. Focus: Mock UserRepository for UserService.findById tests.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: **@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)** why?
conceptual.answer: Integrates Mockito with JUnit 5 lifecycle without JUnit 4 runners. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations.

followUps.question: Production follow-up: which **dashboards** or **metrics** expose this for **JUnit 5 and Mockito**?
followUps.answer: Start with **client** **outbound** **histograms** and **resilience4j** **circuitbreaker** **state** **gauges**, then open **distributed** **traces** filtered by **service** **name** and **route**. Integrates Mockito with JUnit 5 lifecycle without JUnit 4 runners. Contrast **happy** **path** docs with **partition** behavior: **split** **retries**, **half-open** **probes**, **degraded** **read** modes, and **idempotency** keys that prevent **duplicate** **writes**. Mention **verification**: **`curl -v`** through **gateway**, **`kubectl logs`**, **APM** **waterfall** **spans**, and **dashboards** that slice **p99** by **downstream** **service** **name**.

followUps.question: How do you **roll out** a policy change here without a **retry** **storm**?
followUps.answer: Use **feature** **flags**, **canary** **deployments**, **tight** **retry** **budgets** on **non-idempotent** calls, and **verify** with **load** tests that **match** **production** **fan-out**. Integrates Mockito with JUnit 5 lifecycle without JUnit 4 runners. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work.

conceptual.question: **when** vs **doAnswer**?
conceptual.answer: Simple stubbing vs custom logic or side effects in mocks. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads.

followUps.answer: Start with **client** **outbound** **histograms** and **resilience4j** **circuitbreaker** **state** **gauges**, then open **distributed** **traces** filtered by **service** **name** and **route**. Simple stubbing vs custom logic or side effects in mocks. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings.

conceptual.question: **ArgumentCaptor** **use**?
conceptual.answer: Inspect complex arguments passed to collaborators when matchers alone are awkward. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast. sections.title: Cheatsheet
sections.content:
| API | Recall |
|---|---|
| @Mock | Collaborator |
| @InjectMocks | SUT wiring |
| when | Stub return |
| verify | Interaction |
| captor | Arg inspect |
| assertThrows | Exceptions |
| strict | Unused stubs |
| lenient | Rare relax |
| spy | Partial real |
| inline | Mock maker |
